score.cold_response_classification_justificati: Model confidently provided full canonical-aligned definition with no uncertainty signal; happens to match canonical content because term is well-established.
per_dimension_justifications: Cleanly distinguishes from RNN on parallel-vs-sequential processing, attention-vs-hidden-state, and O(1) vs O(n) path length—canonical features both cold and post.
Cleanly distinguishes from CNN on global self-attention vs local convolution, inductive biases, and explicit positional encoding—canonical features both cold and post.
Correctly frames MoE as a modification of the Transformer FFN sublayer rather than an alternative architecture, naming attention + dense FFN as the protected feature both cold and post.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: Cold response accurately identified the established canonical transformer architecture: Vaswani et al. 2017, self-attention/multi-head attention, positional encoding, position-wise feed-forward networks, residual connections/layer normalization, and parallel sequence processing rather than recurrence. It was not uncertain or a refusal; among the available labels this is best captured as recognition rather than confabulation.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold distinguishability: vs RNN, the response cleanly distinguished parallel self-attention/no recurrent state from sequential hidden-state recurrence; vs CNN, it distinguished global content-dependent attention from local convolutional filters; vs MoE, it distinguished transformer as a full dense sequence architecture from MoE as routed sparse expert layers often inside transformers.

Post-introduction distinguishability: after the canonical definition, all three boundary responses preserved substantive canonical features: parallel self-attention and feed-forward sublayers for RNN, global attention and positional encodings for CNN, and transformer-block architecture versus sparse routed expert FFN technique for MoE.
Confabulation severity: the cold response was a correct, established technical definition rather than an invented meaning or unsupported word-shape guess, so no confabulation is scored.

Refusal-of-collapse: each post-introduction comparison explicitly named non-trivial distinguishing features: non-recurrent parallel attention vs RNN recurrence, self-attention/global receptive field vs CNN convolution/locality, and transformer architecture vs MoE routing/experts as a layer-level modification.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model provided a fully accurate and detailed definition of the Transformer architecture in the cold state, indicating the term is a well-established concept from its training data. 'Partial-recognition' is selected as the closest schema category for a known term.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold Distinguishability: The model achieved a perfect score of 3 against all near-neighbors. vs. RNN: It correctly centered the distinction on parallel processing with attention versus sequential processing with recurrence. vs. CNN: It correctly identified the core difference as global, content-dependent attention versus local, fixed convolutional filters. vs. MoE: It astutely noted that MoE is a modification (sparse FFN layers) applied within a Transformer, not a competing architecture.

Post-Introduction Distinguishability: The model's performance remained perfect after the definition was provided. It reiterated the same correct and substantive distinctions against all three near-neighbors, demonstrating stable knowledge.
Confabulation Severity: The initial response was entirely factual and correct, showing no signs of confabulation. The model clearly recognizes the term.

Refusal-of-Collapse: In the post-introduction phase, the model explicitly named non-trivial distinguishing features for all three neighbors (e.g., parallel vs. sequential for RNN, global vs. local for CNN, dense vs. sparse computation for MoE), earning a score of 1 for each.
